etymology.description: semantic 戊 (depicts a weapon) + phonetic 丁 (sound) -> meaning related to protection or completion
etymology.interpretation: Originally related to 'city walls', protected by a weapon (戊), which conveys the idea of completeness or fortification. The sound provided by 丁 reinforces this association. This concept of protection leads to the general sense of something being complete or finished.

examples.chinese: 这本书终于写成了(這本書終於寫成了)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū zhōngyú xiě chéng le.

examples.chinese: 他成功地解决了这个问题(他成功地解決了這個問題)。
examples.pinyin: Tā chénggōng de jiějué le zhège wèntí.

examples.chinese: 我的梦想是成为一名医生(我的夢想是成為一名醫生)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ de mèngxiǎng shì chéngwéi yī míng yīshēng.
examples.english: My dream is to become a doctor.
